claims.claim_text: A platform engineer at a payments company reviewed a production incident caused by divergent copied manifests.
claims.anchor_text: A platform engineer at a payments company once reviewed a production incident that looked boring at first: one service had three slightly different Deployment manifests across development, staging, and production. The staging file had the new resource limits, the production file had the old image tag, and the development file had a debug environment variable that should never have left a sandbox. Nobody had intentionally created a risky deployment system. They had simply copied YAML when the team was small, then kept copying it as the business grew.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A common deployment risk starts with ordinary-looking drift: one service can end up with slightly different Deployment manifests across development, staging, and production. One environment may carry newer resource limits, another may still point at an older image tag, and another may retain a debug setting that should never leave a sandbox. Nobody has to design a risky deployment system intentionally; repeated copy-paste can create one gradually.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed incident-style anecdote, so the rewrite removes the implied real event while preserving the operational lesson.

claims.claim_text: Helm packages Kubernetes applications as charts with templates, defaults, dependencies, and release history.
claims.rationale: The Helm chart documentation describes chart structure, templates, values, dependencies, and chart packaging.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize keeps Kubernetes YAML recognizable and applies transformations through bases, overlays, patches, generators, and image substitutions.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize task page documents resources, overlays, generators, patches, images, and kubectl integration.

claims.claim_text: A Helm chart contains metadata, default values, Kubernetes templates, helper templates, tests, and dependency declarations.
claims.rationale: The Helm chart documentation lists the standard chart files and their purposes.

claims.claim_text: In Chart.yaml, version is the chart package version and appVersion is the application version.
claims.rationale: The Helm chart documentation defines Chart.yaml fields including version and appVersion.

claims.claim_text: Unquoted YAML integers and floats are typically parsed as numeric types, while quoted values are treated as strings.
claims.span_hint: section 2, Active check after helm template example
claims.rationale: Helm's YAML techniques appendix explicitly describes scalar type inference for unquoted and quoted values.

claims.claim_text: Helm stores release information in the cluster and supports install, upgrade, rollback, get, list, and uninstall workflows.
claims.rationale: The Helm v3 FAQ explains in-cluster release records after Tiller removal and Helm command docs cover release operations.

claims.claim_text: A values.schema.json file lets Helm validate chart values before rendering and can catch missing fields, wrong types, ranges, and disallowed values.
claims.rationale: Helm's schema file documentation describes JSON Schema validation for values.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize starts with ordinary Kubernetes YAML plus kustomization.yaml and supports bases, overlays, namespace assignment, prefixes, labels, annotations, patches, images, generators, replacements, and components.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize documentation covers the listed kustomization fields and transformations.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize ConfigMap and Secret generators can add content hashes to generated resource names.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize task page documents generators and generated names with suffix hashes.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize is built into kubectl, enabling commands such as kubectl apply -k and kubectl diff -k.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes documentation states that kubectl supports Kustomize workflows and shows apply -k usage.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize commonLabels may affect selectors as well as metadata, while labels with includeSelectors: false can avoid selector changes.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize documentation covers common labels and label transformer behavior.

claims.claim_text: Deployment selectors are immutable after creation.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Deployment documentation states that .spec.selector is immutable after creation.

claims.claim_text: Argo CD supports Helm sources and Helm values configuration.
claims.rationale: The Argo CD Helm user guide documents using Helm charts and values in Argo CD Applications.

claims.claim_text: Argo CD supports Kustomize sources.
claims.rationale: The Argo CD Kustomize user guide documents Kustomize applications and configuration.

claims.claim_text: Flux HelmRelease supports Kustomize post-renderers for Helm releases.
claims.rationale: The Flux HelmRelease documentation directly documents postRenderers with Kustomize patches and images.

claims.claim_text: Jsonnet/Tanka uses programmable data composition for Kubernetes configuration.
claims.rationale: The Tanka repository describes it as flexible reusable Kubernetes configuration based on Jsonnet.

claims.claim_text: Helm and Kustomize are dominant in most Kubernetes organizations.
claims.anchor_text: In most Kubernetes organizations, Helm and Kustomize are still the dominant tools, so you should learn them first and treat Jsonnet as a specialized pattern you may encounter in mature platform repositories.
claims.suggested_rewrite: This module focuses on Helm and Kustomize first because they are common Kubernetes configuration tools; treat Jsonnet as a specialized pattern you may encounter in mature platform repositories.
claims.rationale: The prevalence claim is uncited and not backed by a current adoption statistic, so the rewrite removes the unsupported dominance claim.

claims.claim_text: A healthcare software team had a Helm chart complexity incident involving disabled encryption.
claims.anchor_text: A healthcare software team let a Helm chart grow from a simple package into a large, hard-to-review set of values and conditionals. The chart supported a sensitive application, and its growing complexity made deployments hard to review confidently. Nobody set out to disable encryption. They created enough nested configuration that reviewers stopped being able to prove encryption was always enabled.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A Helm chart can grow from a simple package into a large, hard-to-review set of values and conditionals. When a chart supports sensitive workloads, that complexity can make deployments hard to review confidently. Nobody has to intend to disable a required control; enough nested configuration can make reviewers unable to prove the control is always enabled.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed war story in a regulated domain, so the rewrite removes the implied real incident.

claims.claim_text: Staging explicitly set every nested field, production omitted a key, and the rendered manifest disabled encryption.
claims.anchor_text: The deployment worked in staging because staging values explicitly set every nested field. Production values omitted one nested key because the team believed the chart default would protect them. The template did not behave the way the reviewer assumed, and the rendered manifest disabled a security requirement that should never have been configurable in the first place.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A risky version of this pattern is easy to imagine: one environment supplies every nested value, another omits a key, and the template default does not behave the way the reviewer assumed. The rendered manifest can then violate a security requirement that should never have been configurable in the first place.
claims.rationale: This continues the unattributed incident narrative, so the rewrite removes the asserted real sequence while keeping the design warning.

claims.anchor_text: Tuesday 2:00 PM      Developer adds a feature flag to the chart
claims.suggested_rewrite: Step 1               A chart change adds new configuration behavior
claims.rationale: The precise weekday and time are fake precision in an unattributed incident timeline.

claims.claim_text: Tuesday 2:30 PM shallow review timeline entry.
claims.anchor_text: Tuesday 2:30 PM      Pull request approved after shallow values review
claims.suggested_rewrite: Step 2               Review focuses on values, but misses template behavior
claims.rationale: The precise weekday and time are fake precision in an unattributed incident timeline.

claims.anchor_text: Tuesday 3:00 PM      Staging deployment succeeds with explicit values
claims.suggested_rewrite: Step 3               One environment succeeds because it supplies explicit values
claims.rationale: The precise weekday and time are fake precision in an unattributed incident timeline.

claims.anchor_text: Tuesday 4:00 PM      Production deployment begins with partial values
claims.suggested_rewrite: Step 4               Another environment deploys with partial values

claims.anchor_text: Tuesday 4:02 PM      Pods start with encryption disabled by template logic

claims.claim_text: Wednesday 9:00 AM security audit detects unencrypted sensitive output timeline entry.
claims.anchor_text: Wednesday 9:00 AM    Security audit detects unencrypted sensitive output
claims.suggested_rewrite: Step 7               A later review detects sensitive output that should have been protected

claims.anchor_text: Wednesday 9:30 AM    Incident response begins and production is restricted

claims.anchor_text: Wednesday 6:00 PM    Encryption restored after chart simplification

claims.claim_text: Following weeks audit, notification, remediation, and retraining timeline entry.
claims.anchor_text: Following weeks      Audit, notification, remediation, and retraining
claims.suggested_rewrite: Afterward            The team audits the failure, remediates the design, and updates review practices
claims.rationale: The timeline implies a real regulated incident lifecycle without attribution.

claims.claim_text: Helm v3 removed Tiller and eliminated the Helm v2 server-side component that raised RBAC/security concerns.
claims.rationale: Helm's official Helm 3 changes page documents Tiller removal and the RBAC/security motivation.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize is built into kubectl, so kubectl apply -k can apply overlays without a separate Kustomize binary in many workflows.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize task page documents kubectl's -k support.

claims.claim_text: Helm charts can include values.schema.json so invalid configuration can fail during rendering.
claims.rationale: Helm's schema file documentation supports the values.schema.json validation claim.

claims.claim_text: Tanka solves a related Kubernetes configuration problem using Jsonnet-style programmable data composition.
claims.rationale: The Tanka repository describes Tanka as reusable Kubernetes configuration using Jsonnet.

further_reading.title: Helm Charts
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary Helm reference for chart structure, values, templates, dependencies, and package metadata.

further_reading.title: Helm YAML Techniques
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains YAML scalar typing and quoting behavior that affects Helm values and rendered manifests.

further_reading.title: Declarative Management with Kustomize
further_reading.why_relevant: Official Kubernetes documentation for Kustomize bases, overlays, generators, labels, images, and kubectl -k workflows.

further_reading.title: Flux HelmRelease Post Renderers
further_reading.why_relevant: Documents the HelmRelease Kustomize post-renderer pattern used for Helm-plus-Kustomize GitOps workflows.

further_reading.title: Helm 3 Changes Since Helm 2
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for Tiller removal, Helm 3 release storage, and values.schema.json validation behavior.

notes: Most tool capability claims can be supported with official Helm, Kubernetes, Argo CD, Flux, and Tanka documentation. The two incident-style narratives are unattributed and should be rewritten as generalized failure patterns rather than cited.
